moduleDescription:
Types and operations for atomic operations and lockless algorithms.

Unstable API.

By default, C++ uses C11 atomic primitives. To use C++ std::atomic, -d:nimUseCppAtomics can be defined.

entries.name: MemoryOrder
entries.code:
MemoryOrder {.importcpp: "std::memory_order".} = enum
  moRelaxed, ## No ordering constraints. Only the atomicity and ordering against
              ## other atomic operations is guaranteed.
  moConsume, ## This ordering is currently discouraged as it's semantics are
              ## being revised. Acquire operations should be preferred.
  moAcquire, ## When applied to a load operation, no reads or writes in the
              ## current thread can be reordered before this operation.
  moRelease, ## When applied to a store operation, no reads or writes in the
              ## current thread can be reorderd after this operation.
  moAcquireRelease, ## When applied to a read-modify-write operation, this behaves like
                     ## both an acquire and a release operation.
  moSequentiallyConsistent ## Behaves like Acquire when applied to load, like Release when
                           ## applied to a store and like AcquireRelease when applied to a
                           ## read-modify-write operation.
                           ## Also guarantees that all threads observe the same total ordering
                           ## with other moSequentiallyConsistent operations.
entries.description: Specifies how non-atomic operations can be reordered around atomic operations.

entries.name: Atomic
entries.code: Atomic[T] {.importcpp: "std::atomic", completeStruct.} = object
entries.description: An atomic object with underlying type T.

entries.name: AtomicFlag
entries.code: AtomicFlag {.importcpp: "std::atomic_flag", size: 1.} = object
entries.description: An atomic boolean state.

entries.name: load
entries.description: Atomically obtains the value of the atomic object.

entries.name: store
entries.description: Atomically replaces the value of the atomic object with the desired value.

entries.name: exchange
entries.description: Atomically replaces the value of the atomic object with the desired value and returns the old value.

entries.name: compareExchange
entries.description: Atomically compares the value of the atomic object with the expected value and performs exchange with the desired one if equal or load if not. Returns true if the exchange was successful.

entries.name: compareExchange
entries.description: Same as above, but allows for different memory orders for success and failure.

entries.name: compareExchangeWeak
entries.description: Same as above, but is allowed to fail spuriously.

entries.name: compareExchangeWeak
entries.description: Same as above, but allows for different memory orders for success and failure.

entries.name: fetchAdd
entries.description: Atomically adds a value to the atomic integer and returns the original value.

entries.name: fetchSub
entries.description: Atomically subtracts a value to the atomic integer and returns the original value.

entries.name: fetchAnd
entries.description: Atomically replaces the atomic integer with it's bitwise AND with the specified value and returns the original value.

entries.name: fetchOr
entries.description: Atomically replaces the atomic integer with it's bitwise OR with the specified value and returns the original value.

entries.name: fetchXor
entries.description: Atomically replaces the atomic integer with it's bitwise XOR with the specified value and returns the original value.

entries.name: testAndSet
entries.description: Atomically sets the atomic flag to true and returns the original value.

entries.name: clear
entries.description: Atomically sets the value of the atomic flag to false.

entries.name: fence
entries.description: Ensures memory ordering without using atomic operations.

entries.name: signalFence
entries.description: Prevents reordering of accesses by the compiler as would fence, but inserts no CPU instructions for memory ordering.

entries.name: atomicInc
entries.code: proc atomicInc[T: SomeInteger](location: var Atomic[T]; value: T = 1) {.inline.}
entries.description: Atomically increments the atomic integer by some value.

entries.name: atomicDec
entries.code: proc atomicDec[T: SomeInteger](location: var Atomic[T]; value: T = 1) {.inline.}
entries.description: Atomically decrements the atomic integer by some value.

entries.code: proc `+=`[T: SomeInteger](location: var Atomic[T]; value: T) {.inline.}
entries.description: Atomically increments the atomic integer by some value.

entries.code: proc `-=`[T: SomeInteger](location: var Atomic[T]; value: T) {.inline.}
entries.description: Atomically decrements the atomic integer by some value.
